generalities.text: Main THE ENCYCLOPEDIA OF PURE MATERIA MEDICA By TIMOTHY F. ALLEN, A.M., M.D. Presented by Médi-T ACTÆA SPICATA. Actæa spicata, L. Nat. order , Ranunculaceæ. Common names , Baneberry, Herb Christopher. Preparation , Tincture from the root obtained in the fall. Authorities. 1 , Petroz, Journ. d. l. Soc. Gal., 3, 12; 2 , Linnæus, from Roth's M. M. 3, 228; 3 , Colden, ibid.; 4 , Lemercier, ibid. MIND. ► Furious delirium , [2] . ► Delirium during fever, [1] . ► Loss of consciousness, [1] . HEAD. ► Boring pain in the head, [1] . ► Headache, which persists after the fever, [1] . ► Pressure in the forehead, from having been in broad daylight, [1] . ► Twitching pain in the temples, [1] . ► Pressure in the vertex, [1] . ► Hammering pain in occiput, [1] . ► Pain which seems seated in the periosteum and even in the bones of the skull, [1] . ► The symptoms are, in general, more intense at night; they are aggravated by walking; for the most part, they return periodically, [1] . ► Sensation of horripilation on the scalp, [1] . ► Eruption of small pimples on the hairy scalp, [2] . ► Warm sweat of the head, [1] . EYES. ► Suggillations of the conjunctiva, [1] . ► Ophthalmia, like catarrhal inflammation, [1] . ► Flow of burning tears, [1] . ► Objects seem colored blue, [1] . ► Spots before eyes when looking steadily, [1] . EARS. ► The ear is sore to the touch, [1] . ► Twitching pain in ears when sneezing, or blowing nose, [1] . ► Murmuring in the ears after sleeping; it becomes worse from mental anxiety, [1] . NOSE. ► Bruised pain in nose, [1] . ► Redness of the nostril, [1] . ► The nasal mucus in tinged with blood, [1] . ► Nasal hemorrhage, during the oppression of the chest, [1] . FACE. ► The skin around the mouth becomes slightly yellowish, [1] . ► The cheek on which he lies perspires easily, [1] . ► *Pain as of rheumatism in the face [1] . MOUTH. ► The lips are somewhat chapped, [1] . ► The salivary secretion is increased, [1] . ► Slightly fetid odor from the mouth, [1] . THROAT. ► The submaxillary glands are painful when chewing, [1] . ► The throat becomes sore when speaking, [1] . ► Tearing pains in the throat, especially when breathing cold morning or evening air, [1] . STOMACH. ► Hunger, with aversion to food, [1] . ► Sharp appetite in the morning, [1] . ► Nausea, [1] . ► Vomiting, [1] . ► Desire to vomit, with dizziness, [1] . ► Vomiting of sour matters, [1] . ► Uneasiness after a meal, [1] . ► Cramp of the stomach, [1] . ► Painful of pressure at pit of stomach, [1] . ► Drawing in the epigastric region, [1] . ABDOMEN. ► Beatings in right hypochondrium, [1] . ► Throbbing in right hypochondrium, [1] . ► The right hypochondrium is painful to pressure, [1] . ► The left hypochondrium becomes almost insensible, [1] . ► Spasmodic contractions of the bowels, [1] . ► Uneasiness in bowels, [1] . ► Feeling of compression in abdomen, [1] . ► Pain in the abdomen like that which precedes or accompanies menstruation, [1] . ► Pain in the bowels like that which precedes diarrha, [1] . ► Copious emission of flatulence, [1] . STOOL AND ANUS. ► Pappy stool, [1] . ► The alvine evacuations are suspended, [4] . URINARY ORGANS. ► Frequent urging to urinate, [2] . ► Frequent urging and painful micturition, [1] . ► The urine deposits a white sediment, [1] . RESPIRATORY APPARATUS. ► Difficult inspiration, with stitching pain in the epigastrium when drawing a deep breath, [1] . ► Difficult breathing, with painful shock in the abdomen at each inspiration, [1] . ► Difficult breathing with pain in stomach, [1] . ► Difficult breathing, with pain in hip, [1] . ► Breathing seems difficult, on account of weakness, especially when expiring air, [1] . BACK. ► Throbbing sensation in the region of the kidneys, [1] . ► Bruised pain in sacral region, when lying on the side, [1] . EXTREMITIES IN GENERAL. ►► *Swelling of the joints after slight fatigue [1] . UPPER EXTREMITIES. ► *Pain as from paralytic weakness in the hands , [1] . ► The fingers are numb and cold; they are discolored, [1] . LOWER EXTREMITIES. ► Swelling of the lower limbs, [1] . ► Boring pain in the lower limbs, ameliorated by stretching them, [1] . ► Weakness of the lower extremities, after changes of temperature, [1] . ► Trembling in the thighs when lifting them, [1] . ► Sensation of great weariness in the knees, [1] . GENERALITIES. ► Lassitude after eating, [1] . ► Sudden lassitude, without apparent cause, [1] . ► Debility from walking in the open air, [1] . ► Easily tired by much talking, [1] . ► Throbbing in the body, [1] . ► Fermentation of the blood, [1] . ► The pains of Actæa spicata are, in general, tearing , drawing, [1] . FEVER. ► Horripilation after drinking, [1] . ► Horripilation followed by heat, during which vomiting supervenes, [1] . ► Viscid sweat, [1] . ► Cold perspiration and malaise, [3] . CONDITIONS. ► Aggravation. ► ( Morning ), Sharp appetite; numbness of right finger-tips. ► (Towards evening), Viscid sweat. ► ( When walking in open air ), Debility. ► ( When drawing deep breath ), Difficult inspiration, etc. ► ( At each inspiration ), Painful shock in abdomen. ► ( When expiring air ), Breathing seems difficult, etc. ► ( Breathing cold morning or evening air ), Tearing pains in throat. ► ( When chewing ), Submaxillary glands painful. ► ( After drinking ), Horripilation. ► ( From having been in broad daylight ), Pressure in forehead. ► ( After eating ), Lassitude. ► ( After a meal ), Uneasiness. ► ( After slight fatigue ), Swelling of the joints. ► ( When looking steadily ), Spots before eyes. ► ( When lying in bed ), Bruised pains in sacral region. ► ( Mental anxiety ), Murmuring in the ears. ► ( During oppression of chest ), Nasal hemorrhage. ► ( Periodically ), Symptoms return. ► ( On pressure ), Right hypochondrium painful. ► ( Sneezing, or blowing nose ), Twitching pain in ears. >> ( After sleeping ), Murmuring in ears. ► ( When speaking ), Throat becomes sore. ► ( Much talking ), Easily tired by. ► ( After changes of temperature ), Weakness of lower extremities. ► ( Walking ), Head symptoms. ► Amelioration. ► ( Stretching lower limbs ), Boring pain in them. Clinical: Cancer of stomach Fright, effects of, Hepatitis; Pleurisy; Rheumatism; Toothache

Difficult inspiration; stitches in epigastrium during deep breathing or a painful shock; or pain in the hip. Swelling of joints after slight fatigue (after walking). Rheumatism of small joints (ankle, toe-joints, and esp. joints of hands). Pain as from paralytic weakness of hands.-Lameness of r. arm.-R. wrist pains intolerably, swollen, red, touch intolerable; motion impossible; pressure on palm intolerable.

head.text: Fearful, starts easily; confused. Ebullition of blood to head excited by drinking coffee. Vertigo, tearing headache, better in open air, throbbing in brain, pain from crown to between eyebrows; heat in forehead, pain in left frontal eminence as if bone were crushed. Itching of scalp alternating with heat; nose red at tip, fluent coryza

A kind of drunkenness. Dizziness; forehead feels empty when stooping. Twitching in temples; pain from a decayed tooth to temples. Pressure in forehead after having been in sun. Head symptoms at night; from walking; return periodically; also after fever. Warm sweat on head. Small pimples on scalp.

face.text: Violent pain in upper jaw, running from teeth through malar bones to temples. Perspiration on face and head

Pains as of rheumatism in face; violent tearing and drawing pains in the upper jaw; pulling, tearing from a decayed tooth to temples; from slightest touch or movement of the muscles. The cheek he lies on sweats. Yellow round mouth.-Lips chapped. Submaxillary glands hurt when chewing.

stomach_abdomen.text: Tearing, darting pains in epigastric region, with vomiting. Cramp-like pains in stomach and epigastrium, with difficult breathing; sense of suffocation. Sudden lassitude after eating
Spasmodic retraction. Sticking pain and distension of hypogastrium

After drinking, shuddering. Sour vomiting. Tearing, darting pain in epigastric region with vomiting. Cancer of the stomach, with characteristic pains. tearing, drawing, &c. Pain and tenderness in liver region.

respiratory.text: Short, irregular breathing at night, while lying. Great oppression. Shortness of breath on exposure to cold air

extremities.text: Tearing pains in loins. Rheumatic pains in small joints, wrist, (Ulmus) fingers, ankles, toes. Swelling of joints from slight fatigue. Wrist swollen, red, worse any motion. Paralytic weakness in the hands. Lame feeling in arms. Pain in knee. Sudden lassitude after talking or eating

keynotes.text: color="#800000">ACTAEA SPICATA Baneberry (ACTEA SPICATA) Is a rheumatic remedy, especially of the small joints; tearing, tingling pains characterize it. Wrist-rheumatism. Pulsations over whole body, especially liver and renal region. Cardiovascular spasm. Pains worse from touch and motion.

This is a rheumatic remedy like the other Actæa, but affecting more prominently the small joints and wrists. I have several times cured wrist-rheumatism with it. The joints swell after slight fatigue. The pains are tearing, drawing. The right arm and right wrist are specially affected; and there are pains like rheumatism in face. Pains are of a violent, tearing, drawing character, by touch or movement. Pains of this kind running from decayed tooth to temples. Swelling of the joints from slight fatigue may be regarded as very characteristic. It is especially suited to men (Act. r. to women). From motion; from change of temperature; cold air.

The black, creeping root-stock is perennial, sending up each year erect stems, growing 1 to 2 feet high, which are triangular and either not branched, or very sparingly so. The foot-stalks of the leaves are long and arise from the root. These divide into three smaller foot-stalks, and are so divided or re-divided that each leaf is composed of eighteen, or even twenty-seven, lobes or leaflets. The flower-stem arises from the roots and has leaves of the same form, but smaller. The flowers grow in spikes and are of a pure white. The whole plant is dark green and glabrous (without hairs), or only very slightly downy. It flowers in June and in autumn ripens its fruits, which are egg-shaped berries, 1/2 inch long, black and shining, many-seeded and very poisonous, well justifying the popular name of Baneberry. The plant is of an acrid, poisonous nature throughout, and though the root has been used in some nervous cases, and is said to be a remedy for catarrh, it must be administered with great caution.

KEY-NOTES TO THE MATERIA MEDICA by HENRY N. GUERNSEY, M.D. Presented by Médi-T ACTÆA SPICATA The indications for this remedy are decidedly rheumatic. Face.-- Violent tearing and drawing pains in the upper jaw, running from the teeth, up through the malar bones, to the temples. Stomach.-- Cancer of the stomach, with characteristic pains-tearing, drawing, etc. Extremities.-- We find it particularly indicated in rheumatic pains in the small joints, wrist, finger, ankle, toe joints. The pains are of a violent, tearing, drawing character, aggravated by contact or movement. Wrist joints pain intolerably, swollen, red, motion impossible, touch intolerable. Sometimes these rheumatic pains become general, though typified in these small joints, as the key-note. Small joints, or all the joints, swell after walking. Pain as from paralytic weakness in the hands. Copyright © Médi-T 2000

_raw: It follows well: Nux v. Compare: Other Ranunculaceæ; Arn., Bry., Caulo. (rheumatism of fingers), Lyc., Rhus t., Salic. ac., and Sticta (small joints).

mental_emotional.text: Fear of death, esp. at night in bed. Furious delirium; also during the fever. Effects of exertion of mind; fright mental anxiety.

eyes.text: Objects seem coloured blue. Sharp pains through eyes into head.

ears.text: Twitching pain in ears when blowing nose or sneezing.

mouth_throat.text: Increased salivation. Fetid odour. Throat sore when speaking; when breathing cold air.

urinary.text: Throbbing in region of kidneys. Urine deposits a white sediment. Urinary calculi.

female.text: Menses suppressed by fright; by cold.

chest_heart.text: Urging sensation in heart towards abdomen and region of liver, with great anxiety at night. Pulse 120.

fever_chill.text: Shuddering after drinking. Belching during the chill. Shuddering followed by heat, during which vomiting supervenes. Copyright © Médi-T ® 2000 Main.

habitat.text: It is to be found in copses on limestone in Yorkshire and the Lake District, but is so uncommon as to be regarded by some botanists as almost a doubtful native. The Baneberry, or Herb Christopher, is a rather rare British plant belonging (like the Paeony) to the Buttercup order, but distinguished from all other species in the order by its berry-like fruit. It is considered to have similar anti-spasmodic properties to the Paeony.

indications.text: Antispasmodic. The juice of the berries, mixed with alum, yields a black dye. There are two varieties of this species, one of British origin, only distinguished from the rest of the species by its berries being red, instead of black and the other an American plant ( Actaea alba , or White Cohosh) with white berries. Both varieties grow in the writer's garden. The American species is considered by the natives a valuable remedy against snake-bite, especially of the rattlesnake, hence it is - with several other plants - sometimes known as one of the 'Rattlesnake herbs.' It is said the name 'Herb Christopher' was also formerly applied to the flowering fern, Osmunda regalis . The name of the genus is from the Greek acte , the elder, which these plants resemble as regards the leaves and berries. Toads seem to be attracted by the smell of the Baneberry, which causes it also to be termed Toadroot, the name arising possibly also from its preference for the damp shady situations in which the toad is found. It is also called Bugbane, because of its offensive smell, which is said to drive away vermin. Closely allied to this plant, and at one time assigned to the same genus, is the plant known as Black Cohosh. See (BLACK) COHOSH . [ Top ]
